top_categories.workout_context: 13,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
123ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At La Vaujany Cyclosportive, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
La Vaujany Cyclosportive's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 106 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic La Vaujany Cyclosportive's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
At mile 60 of 106, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of La Vaujany Cyclosportive. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 106 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
13,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At La Vaujany Cyclosportive, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Low-cadence force work for 13,000ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 13,000ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for La Vaujany Cyclosportive’s 106 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: La Vaujany Cyclosportive with 13,000ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at La Vaujany Cyclosportive requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 106 miles of mixed terrain in Vaujany, Isère, France.
